name: Update EInvoiceSaaSCommunication.Codeunit.al

diff:

+++ b/App/Layers/NA/BaseApp/Local/eServices/EDocument/EInvoiceSaaSCommunication.Codeunit.al
@@ -111,7 +111,7 @@ codeunit 10175 "EInvoice SaaS Communication" implements "EInvoice Communication
     end;
 
     [NonDebuggable]
-    local procedure GetAzFunctionSecrets(var ClientID: Text; var Certificate: SecretText; var Scope: Text; var Endpoint: Text; var AuthUrl: Text)
+    procedure GetAzFunctionSecrets(var ClientID: Text; var Certificate: Text; var Scope: Text; var Endpoint: Text; var AuthUrl: Text)   
     var
         AzureKeyVault: Codeunit "Azure Key Vault";
         EnvironmentInformation: Codeunit "Environment Information";
@@ -130,7 +130,7 @@ codeunit 10175 "EInvoice SaaS Communication" implements "EInvoice Communication
     end;
 
     [NonDebuggable]
-    local procedure GetCertificate(var Certificate: SecretText): Boolean;
+    local procedure GetCertificate(var Certificate: Text): Boolean;
     var
         AzureKeyVault: Codeunit "Azure Key Vault";
         CertificateName: Text;

target_comments.comment: Procedure calling Azure Key Vault should not be public.

name: Update BankAccReconTest.Codeunit.al
description: Changes in iteration 2 that received 2 comment(s)
pr_title: Performance improvement for total outstanding amounts in bank rec posting

target_comments.comment:
It looks like we need this kind of SIFT index on Bank Account Ledger Entry (we don't have it)

"Bank Account No.", "Posting Date", "Statement No", "Closed at Date", "Statement Status", Reversed, Open 
SumIndexFields =  Amount
target_comments.author: Djordje Cenic

target_comments.comment: It is more efficient to line up the valid values with OR (|) than say <> InvalidOne. It may be counterintuitive, but according to Torben Wind, it is like that. In the <> case on an enum, even if we have a SIFT index for Amount (which we need to add), having a <> InvalidOne will make it do a full index scan (performs like a table scan with a sum - in other words, no help), whereas a sequence of ORs makes the server fetch the precalculated sums for the individual enum members, much more efficient.
target_comments.author: Djordje Cenic
